dosage.title: Dosage Calculator
dosage.noSelection: Select a drug and enter weight to see dosage.
dosage.disclaimer: Always verify with a qualified clinician

nutrition.title: Nutrition Assessment
nutrition.howStep1: 1. Bend elbow 90°. Mark midpoint between shoulder tip and elbow tip (left arm).
nutrition.howStep2: 2. Relax arm at side. Wrap MUAC tape at midpoint — not too tight or loose.
nutrition.howStep3: 3. Read value where the tape end aligns with the scale (zero mark).
nutrition.pregnantNormalRec: Continue routine antenatal care. Nutritional support advised.
nutrition.pregnantModerateRec: Enrol in targeted supplementary feeding. Monitor monthly.
nutrition.pregnantHighRec: Refer immediately for therapeutic nutritional intervention.
nutrition.edemaRec: Admit to SAM programme. Treat for kwashiorkor.
nutrition.samRec: Refer to SAM programme (OTP/TFC). Routine antibiotics + Vitamin A.
nutrition.mamRec: Enrol in Supplementary Feeding Programme (SFP). Monitor weekly.
nutrition.normalRec: Continue growth monitoring. No intervention required.
nutrition.zSevereRec: Admit to therapeutic feeding programme. Treat underlying cause.
nutrition.zModerateRec: Refer to supplementary feeding programme. Follow up in 4 weeks.
nutrition.zNormalRec: Continue growth monitoring. No intervention required.
nutrition.borderlineNote: Borderline result — verify with clinical assessment
nutrition.disclaimer: Always verify with a qualified clinician

diagnose.title: Diagnostic Check
diagnose.noMatch: No matching protocol. Reassess or refer.
diagnose.disclaimer: Always verify with a qualified clinician
diagnose.dhInstr: Tap each sign present in the patient
diagnose.dhAction_none: Continue breastfeeding / oral fluids. Monitor for changes.
diagnose.dhAction_some: Give ORS: 75 mL/kg over 4 h. Reassess every 30 min.
diagnose.dhAction_severe: Start IV fluids immediately (Ringer's lactate / normal saline). Urgent referral.
diagnose.dhORSDetail: 75 mL/kg over 4 hours — give small, frequent sips
diagnose.pnThreshold: RR threshold for this age: ≥{{n}} bpm
diagnose.pnOutOfRange: WHO IMCI applies to children 0–59 months only
diagnose.pnAction_none: Supportive care. Reassess if condition worsens.
diagnose.pnAction_pneumonia: Start oral amoxicillin (1st line). Reassess in 48 h.
diagnose.pnAction_severe: Refer urgently. Oxygen if available. IV/IM antibiotics.
diagnose.mdAction_malaria: Blood smear / RDT for malaria. Treat if positive (ACT first-line).
diagnose.mdAction_dengue: Full blood count (platelets, haematocrit). NS1/IgM if available. IV fluids if warning signs.
diagnose.mdAction_undifferentiated: Malaria RDT + full blood count. Supportive care. Monitor closely for deterioration.
diagnose.nbInstr: Tap each danger sign present in the newborn
diagnose.nb_unableToFeed: Unable to feed or stopped feeding
diagnose.nbAction_danger: Refer immediately to hospital. Keep warm (kangaroo care), continue breastfeeding if possible. Do not delay.
diagnose.nbAction_safe: Continue routine newborn care. Monitor feeding, temperature, and skin colour. Return if any sign develops.

phrases.title: Phrase Cards
phrases.tapToCopy: Tap card to copy · Press ⊞ to show patient

consent.title: Health Screening
consent.body: This screening uses validated clinical tools (PHQ-9, MNA-SF) to identify health risks. No personal information is collected.
consent.point1: Anonymous — no name, age, or contact stored
consent.point2: Results stay on this device until synced
consent.point3: Volunteer use only — not a medical diagnosis

phq9.title: Depression Screening (PHQ-9)
phq9.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following?
phq9.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phq9.q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
phq9.q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
phq9.q6: Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure
phq9.q8: Moving or speaking slowly / being restless or fidgety
phq9.q9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself

mnasf.title: Nutrition Screening (MNA-SF)
mnasf.q1: Has food intake declined over the past 3 months?
mnasf.q2: Weight loss during the last 3 months?
mnasf.q3_1: Able to get out of bed/chair, but does not go out
mnasf.q4: Psychological stress or acute disease in the past 3 months?

result.title: Screening Results
result.highRiskCondition: High-risk condition detected — referral strongly recommended

s1.title: Welcome to GraceBridge
s1.desc: Empowering church volunteers to screen vulnerable community members for health risks and connect them to nearby care.

s2.title: Three Screening Tools
s2.desc: PHQ-9 for depression, MNA-SF for nutrition, and a chronic conditions checklist — all based on validated clinical standards.

s3.title: Privacy by Design
s3.desc: No names, ages, or contact information are ever collected. Only anonymous scores are stored to protect those you serve.

onboarding.consent: I understand this tool is for volunteer screening only, not a medical diagnosis. No personal identifying information will be collected.

auth.subtitle: Bridging Faith and Health
auth.title: Enter Organization Code
auth.desc: Enter the 6-digit code provided by your organization coordinator.
auth.privacy: No personal information is collected. Only anonymous screening data is stored.
